notable_excerpts: The profit after tax for the quarter stood at Rs. 676 crores, representing 9.6% of the sales. This includes one-time impact of Rs. 276 crores from new labor code, which is sitting in the exceptional item rather than above EBITDA.
Our consumer health business continued its upward trajectory, with Nicotex, Omnigel, and Cipladine consolidating number one positions in their respective segments.
During the quarter, we entered into a strategic agreement with Pfizer for exclusive marketing and distribution rights of four well-established Pfizer brands in India.
As I prepare to hand over my responsibilities, it is my privilege to invite Achin to take charge and lead the next phase of our growth and journey.

chain_of_thought: The earnings call transcript for Cipla's Q3 FY26 reveals a lack of environmental disclosures, moderate social disclosures focusing on employee costs and community health, and strong governance disclosures highlighting strategic agreements and leadership transition.
strengths: Strong governance with strategic agreements and leadership transition
Focus on community health through consumer health business
weaknesses: Limited detail on social initiatives beyond employee costs
